channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The iPhone 17 Air could be first in line for this cutting-edge display technology
item.description: The iPhone 17 rumors are certainly up and running now – and the latest to reach us is around displays.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Using your Apple Watch to monitor blood pressure
item.description: Checking your blood pressure has become simple with the Apple Watch, and doing this can help you identify trends and manage medications.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The shocking rise and fall of elite cybersecurity firm IronNet
item.description: The future was once dazzling for IronNet. Founded by a former director of the National Security Agency and stacked with elite members of the U.S. intelligence establishment, IronNet promised it was going to revolutionize the way governments and corporations combat cyberattacks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Alphabet’s Waymo will expand its robotaxi fleet with Hyundai EVs
item.description: Alphabet’s Waymo will add electric vehicles from South Korea’s Hyundai Motor to its robotaxi fleet, the companies said on Friday, as Waymo expands in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Civilization 7 makers work with Shawnee to bring sincere representation of the tribe to the game
item.description: MIAMI, Okla. (AP) — Shawnee Tribe Chief Ben Barnes grew up playing video games, including “probably hundreds of hours” colonizing a distant planet in the 1999 title Sid Meier's Alpha Centauri.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The future of EV Charging looks a lot like an airport lounge
item.description: Charging companies and automakers increasingly see a need for stations with amenities: restaurants, good bathrooms, comfortable furniture, and canopies that shield from the rain, snow and sun.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Austrian activist Schrems wins privacy case against Meta over personal data on sexual orientation
item.description: The European Union’s top court has said that social media company Meta can’t use public information about a user’s sexual orientation obtained outside its platforms for personalized advertising under the bloc’s strict data privacy rules.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Google says it will stop linking to New Zealand news if a law passes forcing it to pay for content
item.description: Google says it will stop linking to New Zealand news content and will reverse its support of local media outlets if the government passes a law forcing tech companies to pay for articles displayed on their platforms.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Amazon faces NLRB complaint over refusing to bargain with drivers’ union
item.description: Amazon.com has been accused by a U.S. labor board of illegally refusing to bargain with a union representing drivers employed by a contractor, the agency announced on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: California schools will be required to integrate AI into curriculum
item.description: New California law will require schools to incorporate AI literacy into math, science, and history-social science curricula.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Macasero, The Mercury News

item.title: Google's search engine's latest AI injection will answer voiced questions about video and photos
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is injecting its search engine with more artificial intelligence that will enable people to voice questions about images and occasionally organize an entire page of results, despite the technology's past misadventures with misleading information.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Texas AG Sues TikTok Over Alleged Online Safety and Privacy Violations
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton sues TikTok for allegedly violating a bill protecting children from online harms and failing to prioritize minors' safety and privacy.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: OpenAI demands investors shun rivals such as Anthropic, Elon Musk’s xAI
item.description: OpenAI has raised $6.6 billion from investors like Thrive Capital and Tiger Global, but the AI company also sought assurances that investors would avoid funding five competing firms, according to a Reuters report.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Spider lovers scurry to Colorado town in search of mating tarantulas and community
item.description: It’s tarantula mating season on the Colorado plains, when male spiders scurry out of their burrows in search of a mate.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Peipert And Hannah Schoenbaum, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hurricane Helene brings climate change to forefront of the presidential campaign
item.description: The devastation wrought by Hurricane Helene has brought climate change to the forefront of the presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Spring AI: An AI framework for Java developers
item.description: Artificial intelligence has been something of a fiesta for programmers for the last few years, and one language—Python—has been the undeniable belle of the ball. Java and other languages have been a bit sidelined.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 23 having arrived September 17, work already has begun on JDK 24, with three features so far proposed for the release: warnings to prepare developers for future restrictions on the use of JNI (Java Native Interface) and a late barrier expansion for the G1 garbage collector
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Large language models hallucinating non-existent developer packages could fuel supply chain attacks
item.description: Large Language Models (LLMs) have a serious “package hallucination” problem that could lead to a wave of maliciously-coded packages in the supply chain, researchers have discovered in one of the largest and most in-depth ever studies to investigate the problem.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Altera Unveils New FPGA Hardware, Software, and Services at Developer Conference
item.description: Altera, a standalone company focused on FPGA hardware, introduces new products, software, and services at its annual developer conference.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Device42 amps up IT asset management with generative AI
item.description: Device42 today announced new functionality in its IT inventory and asset management virtual appliance that enables IT managers to apply generative AI to query their configuration management database (CMDB) using natural language and more quickly get responses via a chat-like interface.
item.dc:creator: Network World
